title: Rinorea macropyxis
taxonomy: Rinorea macropyxis is classified within the family Violaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the violet family. This classification places it in the order Malpighiales, which is a part of the larger clade of core eudicots. The plant is also a member of the genus Rinorea, a group of flowering plants found primarily in tropical regions. The genus Rinorea is part of the family Violaceae and is commonly known as the violet family. This classification provides a framework for understanding the evolutionary relationships among plants in this group.

intro: Rinorea macropyxis, also known as the "Asian kidney-leaf plant", is a species of flowering plant in the family Violaceae. Traditional medicine has utilized Rinorea macropyxis for its reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Rinorea macropyxis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Rinorea macropyxis is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine to make tonics and remedies for various health conditions.
